00025 /*=========================================================================
00026  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00027  * SUBSYSTEM: main program
00028  * FILE:      main.c
00029  * OVERVIEW:  Runs the Java class verifier.
00030  *=======================================================================*/
00031 
00032 /*=========================================================================
00033  * Include files
00034  *=======================================================================*/
00035 
00036 #include "wrappers.h"
00037 
00038 #include "sys_api.h"
00039 #include "path_md.h"
00040 #include "path.h"
00041 #include "oobj.h"
00042 #include "jar.h"
00043 #include "locale_md.h"
00044 
00045 /*=========================================================================
00046  * Globals and extern declarations
00047  *=======================================================================*/
00048 
00049 char str_buffer[STRINGBUFFERSIZE];   /*  shared string buffer */
00050 
00051 int processedfile = 0;
00052 int errorCode = 0;               /* Error code returned by program */
00053 
00054 bool no_native_methods = false;
00055 bool no_floating_point = false;
00056 bool no_finalizers = false;
00057 char tmp_dir[32];                /* temporary directory */
00058 extern char *output_dir;         /* output directory */
00059 bool tmpDirExists = false;
00060 
00061 extern void VerifyFile(register char *fn);
00062 extern bool ProcessJARfile(char *buf, int len);
00063 extern bool isJARfile(char *fn, int length);
00064 
00065 static void usage(char *progname);
00066 
00067 /*=========================================================================
00068  * FUNCTION:      recurse_dir()
00069  * TYPE:          Handles recursive directories
00070  * OVERVIEW:      Internal function called by ProcessInputs().
00071  *
00072  *  This function reads a directory, searching for either another directory,
00073  *  JAR file or an individual class name that is to be verified.
00074  *
00075  * INTERFACE:
00076  *   parameters:  dirname   name of the directory entry.
00077  *                pkgname   name of the package
00078  *   returns:     nothing
00079  *=======================================================================*/
00080 static void recurse_dir(char *dirname, char *pkgname)
00081 {
00082     struct dirent *ent;
00083         char buf[MAXPACKAGENAME];
00084         char pkgbuf[MAXPACKAGENAME];
00085     DIR *dir = opendir(dirname);
00086 
00087     if (dir == NULL) {
00088         fprintf(stderr, "Can't open dir %s\n", dirname);
00089         exit(1);
00090     }
00091     for (ent = readdir(dir); ent; ent = readdir(dir)) {
00092         struct stat stat_buf;
00093         char *name = ent->d_name;
00094         int len;
00095 
00096         if (strcmp(name, ".") == 0 || strcmp(name, "..") == 0) {
00097             continue;
00098         }
00099 
00100         strcpy(pkgbuf, pkgname);
00101         if (pkgname[0] != 0) {
00102             strcat(pkgbuf, "/");
00103         }
00104 
00105                 if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00106                     jio_fprintf(stderr,
00107             "recurse_dir: Reading filename [%s] from directory [%s]\n",
00108                         name, dirname);
00109 
00110         strcat(pkgbuf, name);
00111 
00112         strcpy(buf, dirname);
00113         strcat(buf, name);
00114 
00115         stat(buf, &stat_buf);
00116         len = strlen(buf);
00117 
00118         if (stat_buf.st_mode & S_IFDIR) {
00119             /* handle the recursive directory found */
00120             strcat(buf, "/");
00121             if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00122                 jio_fprintf(stderr,
00123                    "recurse_dir: Recursive directory found, calling recurse_dir ([%s] [%s])\n",
00124                        buf, pkgbuf);
00125             recurse_dir(buf, pkgbuf);
00126             continue;
00127         } else if (isJARfile (buf, len)) {
00128 
00129             /*
00130                      * this directory contains a JAR file which contains
00131                      * the classes to be verified
00132                      */
00133 
00134                     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00135                     jio_fprintf(stderr,
00136                      "recurse_dir: Found JAR file [%s] in dir!\n", buf);
00137 
00138                 if (!ProcessJARfile(buf, len)) {
00139 
00140                 fprintf(stderr, "Not a valid JAR file [%s]\n", buf);
00141                 exit(1);
00142                 }
00143            }
00144 
00145             /* we just have a class file that needs verification */
00146 
00147         len = strlen(pkgbuf);
00148         if (len > 6 && strcmp(pkgbuf + len - 6, ".class") == 0) {
00149 
00150                     pkgbuf[len - 6] = 0;
00151 
00152                     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00153                     jio_fprintf(stderr,
00154                 "recurse_dir: Verifying Class [%s] \n", pkgbuf);
00155             VerifyFile(pkgbuf);
00156         }
00157     }
00158 
00159     closedir(dir);
00160 }
00161 
00162 /*=========================================================================
00163  * FUNCTION:      ProcessInputs()
00164  * TYPE:          Processes inputs
00165  * OVERVIEW:      Internal function called by main().
00166  *
00167  *  This function processes input entries for specifying classes that are to
00168  *  be verified. The inputs may be in 3 forms: a directory, or nested
00169  *  directories, one or more JAR files, or one or more individual class files.
00170  *  If a directory entry is specified, it may also contain one or more JAR
00171  *  files, or one or more individual class files.
00172  *
00173  * INTERFACE:
00174  *   parameters:  argname:  directory, JAR file or an individual class file.
00175  *   returns:     nothing
00176  *=======================================================================*/
00177 
00178 static void ProcessInputs(char *argname)
00179 {
00180     char buf[MAXPACKAGENAME];
00181     struct stat stat_buf;
00182     int res = stat(argname, &stat_buf);
00183     int len;
00184 
00185 
00186     strcpy(buf, argname);
00187     len = strlen(argname);
00188 
00189 
00190     if ((res == 0) && (stat_buf.st_mode & S_IFDIR)) {
00191         /* Append dir separator if it does not yet exist. */
00192         if (buf[len - 1] != LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR &&
00193         buf[len - 1] != DIR_SEPARATOR) {
00194         buf[len] = DIR_SEPARATOR;
00195         buf[len + 1] = 0;
00196         }
00197         pushDirectoryOntoClassPath(buf);
00198         recurse_dir(buf, "");
00199         popClassPath();
00200     } else if ((res == 0) && (isJARfile (buf, len))) {
00201         /* the classes to be verified are in a JAR file */
00202         if (!ProcessJARfile(buf, len)) {
00203         fprintf(stderr, "Not a valid JAR file [%s]\n", buf);
00204         exit(1);
00205         }
00206     } else {
00207         char *p;
00208         /* Convert all periods in the argname to slashes */
00209         for (p = buf; ((p = strchr(p, '.')) != 0); *p++ = '/');
00210             if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00211             jio_fprintf(stderr,
00212             "ProcessInputs: Verifying file [%s]\n", buf );
00213         VerifyFile(buf);
00214     }
00215 }
00216 
00217 /*=========================================================================
00218  * FUNCTION:      main()
00219  * TYPE:          Runs the verifier.
00220  * OVERVIEW:      Main function.
00221  *
00222  *  This is the main function that invokes the Java class verifier.
00223  *
00224  * INTERFACE:
00225  *   parameters:  argc: arg count.
00226  *                argv: arg value(s)
00227  *   returns:     nothing
00228  *=======================================================================*/
00229 int main(int argc, char *argv[])
00230 {
00231     char *progname;
00232     char *argv0 = argv[0];
00233     #if HAVE_SETENV
00234     setenv("LANG", "C", 1);
00235     #endif
00236     SET_DEFAULT_LOCALE;
00237 
00238     if ((progname = strrchr(argv[0], LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR)) != 0) {
00239     progname++;
00240     } else {
00241     progname = argv[0];
00242     }
00243 
00244     /* initialize tmp_dir */
00245     memset(tmp_dir, 0, 32);
00246 
00247     /* initialize the random number generator */
00248     srand(time(NULL));
00249 
00250     /* generate a random temp directory name */
00251     sprintf(tmp_dir, "%s%d%c", "tmp", rand(), '\0');
00252 
00253     while (--argc > 0)
00254     if ((++argv)[0][0] == '-') {
00255             if (strcmp(argv[0], "-verbose") == 0) {
00256                 extern bool verbose;
00257                 verbose = true;
00258 #ifdef DEBUG_VERIFIER
00259         } else if (strcmp(argv[0], "-verify-verbose") == 0) {
00260         extern int verify_verbose;
00261         verify_verbose++;
00262 #endif
00263             } else if (strcmp(argv[0], "-cldc1.0") == 0) {
00264                 no_native_methods = true;
00265                 no_floating_point = true;
00266                 no_finalizers = true;
00267             } else if (strcmp(argv[0], "-nofinalize") == 0) {
00268                 no_finalizers = true;
00269             } else if (strcmp(argv[0], "-nonative") == 0) {
00270                 no_native_methods = true;
00271             } else if (strcmp(argv[0], "-nofp") == 0) {
00272                 no_floating_point = true;
00273             } else if (strcmp(argv[0], "-Xns") == 0) {
00274                 extern bool stack_map_on;
00275                 stack_map_on = false;
00276             } else if (strcmp(argv[0], "-Xni") == 0) {
00277                 extern bool inline_jsr_on;
00278                 inline_jsr_on = false;
00279             } else if (strcmp(argv[0], "-d") == 0) {
00280                 output_dir = strdup(argv[1]);
00281         argc--; argv++;
00282         } else if (strcmp(argv[0], "-classpath") == 0) {
00283         if (argc > 1) {
00284             char *buf = (char *)malloc(strlen(argv[1]) + 32);
00285             sprintf(buf, "CLASSPATH=%s", argv[1]);
00286             putenv(buf);
00287             argc--; argv++;
00288         } else {
00289             fprintf(stderr,
00290              "-classpath requires class path specification\n");
00291             usage(progname);
00292             exit(1);
00293         }
00294         } else {
00295         fprintf(stderr, "%s: Illegal option %s\n\n", progname, argv[0]);
00296         usage(progname);
00297         exit(1);
00298         }
00299     } else if (argv[0][0] == '@') {
00300             char *new_argv[MAXOPTIONS];
00301             char *buffer;
00302         int new_argc = 1;
00303             char *token;
00304             bool done = false;
00305             struct stat sbuf;
00306         FILE *fp;
00307             int buflen;
00308             int filelength;
00309             int i;
00310 
00311         new_argv[0] = argv0;
00312 
00313         fp = fopen(&argv[0][1], "rt");
00314         if (fp == NULL) {
00315         fprintf(stderr, "Can't open %s\n", &argv[0][1]);
00316         exit(1);
00317         }
00318 
00319             /* get the size of the file */
00320             stat(&argv[0][1], &sbuf);
00321             filelength = sbuf.st_size;
00322 
00323             /* allocate the buffer */
00324             if ((buffer = (char *) malloc(filelength+1)) == NULL) {
00325                 fprintf(stderr, "Out of memory\n");
00326                 exit(1);
00327             }
00328 
00329             if (fgets(buffer, filelength+1, fp) != NULL) {
00330                 buflen = strlen(buffer);
00331 
00332                 if (DEBUG_READFROMFILE) {
00333                     fprintf(stderr, "Buffer = [%s]\n", buffer);
00334                 }
00335                 /* get the first parameter */
00336                 token = strtok(buffer, " \0\n\r\t\"");
00337 
00338                 /* Search for parameters until none can be found */
00339                 while (token != NULL && !done) {
00340 
00341                     if ((strcmp(token, "\n") == 0) ||
00342                         (strcmp(token, "\r") == 0) ||
00343                         (strcmp(token, "\t") == 0)) {
00344                         /* <NL>, <CR> or <TAB> */
00345                         done = true;
00346                     } else {
00347                         /* On Win32, it's possible to have a <CR> <LF>
00348                          * appended at the end of the token, which needs
00349                          * to be extracted before restoring the arg
00350                          */
00351                         char *p;
00352                         /* Convert all <CR> <LF> to NULL */
00353                         for (p = token; *p != '\0'; p++) {
00354                             if (*p == '\n' || *p == '\r')
00355                                 *p = '\0';
00356                         }
00357 
00358                         /* save the parameter */
00359                         new_argv[new_argc++] = token;
00360 
00361                         if ((strcmp(token, "-classpath") == 0) ||
00362                             (strcmp(token, "-d")         == 0)) {
00363                             /* search for the beginning of a string literal */
00364                             token = strtok(NULL, "\"");
00365 
00366                         } else { /* get the next token */
00367                             token = strtok(NULL," \0\n\r\t\"");
00368                         }
00369                     }
00370                 }
00371 
00372                 if (DEBUG_READFROMFILE) {
00373                     /* print the arguments after restoring all of them */
00374                     for (i=0; i < new_argc; i++)
00375                         fprintf(stderr, "new_argv[%d] = [%s]\n", i, new_argv[i]);
00376                 }
00377             }
00378 
00379         fclose(fp);
00380         main(new_argc, new_argv);
00381 
00382             if (buffer != NULL) {
00383                 free(buffer);
00384             }
00385         argc--; argv++;
00386     } else {
00387         processedfile++;
00388             if (strlen(argv[0]) > MAXPACKAGENAME) {
00389         fprintf(stderr,
00390              "Package name for class exceeds max allowed size [1024]\n");
00391         usage(progname);
00392         exit(1);
00393             }
00394 
00395             /* indicate location of the verified classes only for verbose mode */
00396             if (verbose) {
00397                 if (!output_dir) {
00398                     fprintf(stderr, "[Output directory for verified classes: output]\n");
00399                 } else {
00400                     fprintf(stderr, "[Output directory for verified classes: %s]\n",
00401                         output_dir);
00402                 }
00403             }
00404         ProcessInputs(argv[0]);
00405     }
00406     if (processedfile == 0) {
00407     usage(progname);
00408     exit(1);
00409     }
00410     return errorCode;  /* normal errorCode=0 indicates successful completion */
00411 }
00412 
00413 static void usage(char *progname) {
00414     fprintf(stderr, "Usage: %s [options] classnames|dirnames ...\n", progname);
00415     fprintf(stderr, "\n");
00416     fprintf(stderr, "where options include:\n");
00417     fprintf(stderr, "   -classpath     <directories separated by '%c'>\n", (char)PATH_SEPARATOR);
00418     fprintf(stderr, "                  Directories in which to look for classes\n");
00419     fprintf(stderr, "   -d <directory> Directory in which output is written (default is ./output/)\n");
00420 
00421     fprintf(stderr, "   -cldc1.0       Checks for existence of language features prohibited \n");
00422     fprintf(stderr, "                  by CLDC 1.0 (native methods, floating point and finalizers)\n");
00423 
00424     fprintf(stderr, "   -nofinalize    No finalizers allowed\n");
00425     fprintf(stderr, "   -nonative      No native methods allowed\n");
00426     fprintf(stderr, "   -nofp          No floating point operations allowed\n");
00427     fprintf(stderr, "   @<filename>    Read command line arguments from a text file\n");
00428     fprintf(stderr, "                  Command line arguments must all be on a single line\n");
00429     fprintf(stderr, "                  Directory names must be enclosed in double quotes (\")\n");
00430 
00431     fprintf(stderr, "\n");
00432 }
00433 
00434 
00435 #ifndef O_BINARY
00436 #define O_BINARY 0
00437 #endif
00438 
00439 int
00440 OpenCode(char *fn, char *sfn, char *dir, struct stat * st)
00441 {
00442     long codefd;
00443     if (fn == 0 || (codefd = open(fn, O_BINARY, 0644)) < 0
00444         || fstat(codefd, st) < 0)
00445     return -2;
00446     return codefd;
00447 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:26 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
